reasons: Baxia directly reduces the value of Floryn's healing-based teamfight impact.
He can pressure fights while applying anti-heal through his hero mechanics, not only through items.

proof.summary: Floryn wants to extend fights with healing, while Baxia reduces shield and HP regeneration effects when his skills hit enemies.
worksBestWhen: Baxia reaches the heroes Floryn is trying to save.
Baxia starts fights before Floryn's team can reset.
failureCases: Baxia cannot touch Floryn's protected backline.
Floryn heals allies outside Baxia's pressure range.

reasons: Franco can punish Floryn's lack of escape by hooking or suppressing her before she can safely use her healing tools.
Suppression is strong when the goal is to remove the healer before the main fight.

proof.summary: Floryn wants safe backline positioning, while Franco can isolate and suppress her long enough for teammates to burst her.
worksBestWhen: Franco has bush control or vision setup.
The team is ready to follow the hook or suppression.
failureCases: Franco hooks a tank instead of Floryn.
Floryn stays behind minions or frontline and avoids pickoff angles.

reasons: Natalia threatens Floryn from fog and bushes, forcing Floryn to play farther back.
Silence and burst pressure can prevent Floryn from freely casting during a fight.

proof.summary: Floryn is vulnerable when isolated, while Natalia can camouflage from bushes and use an enhanced attack to silence and burst a backline support.
worksBestWhen: Natalia controls side bushes and tracks Floryn's rotation.
Floryn's frontline is separated from her.
failureCases: Floryn's team reveals or bodyguards Natalia's route.
Natalia fails to kill quickly and becomes trapped.

reasons: Saber can delete Floryn if she steps too far forward without defensive tools.
His airborne burst is useful against immobile supports.

proof.summary: Floryn needs to stay alive to heal allies, while Saber can lock and burst her during a short pickoff window.
worksBestWhen: Saber finds Floryn before the main fight begins.
Floryn has no nearby peel or defensive battle spell.
failureCases: Floryn survives the combo and her team collapses on Saber.

reasons: Hayabusa can bypass frontline spacing and pressure Floryn from unexpected angles.
He is strongest when Floryn's team lacks reliable peel or reveal.

proof.summary: Floryn wants to remain protected behind allies, while Hayabusa's shadow mobility lets him reach the backline and force her defensive resources.
worksBestWhen: Hayabusa enters after Floryn's team has used key crowd control.
Minions and extra targets are limited so his burst focuses correctly.
failureCases: Hayabusa's damage splits onto other units.
Floryn is protected by hard crowd control or anti-dive supports.
